name: software
description: Epic-based software development workflow with review loops. Creates epics from a specification, then iteratively implements tickets per epic with code review.

phases.name: Write Specification
phases.prompt:
Based on the user requirements, write a detailed technical specification.

The specification should include:
1. Overview and goals
2. Functional requirements
3. Non-functional requirements (performance, security, etc.)
4. Technical constraints
5. Out of scope items

Save the specification to {{state_dir}}/spec.md

phases.name: Plan Epics
phases.system_prompt:
You are a software architect breaking down a specification into epics (major features).

State directory: {{state_dir}}
Specification: {{state_dir}}/spec.md

Write your output to: {{state_dir}}/epics.json
phases.prompt:
Read the specification at {{state_dir}}/spec.md.

Break it down into epics (major features/milestones). Each epic should be a cohesive unit of functionality that can be completed independently.

Create {{state_dir}}/epics.json with the following structure:
[
  {
    "id": "epic-001",
    "name": "Epic Name",
    "description": "What this epic accomplishes",
    "priority": 1,
    "status": "pending"
  }
]

Order epics by priority (dependencies first, then by business value).

phases.name: Process Epics
Create Tickets for Epic
Implement Tickets
phases.system_prompt:
Implementing ticket {{ticket.id}} for epic {{epic.id}}

Ticket directory: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/

phases.name: Implement Ticket
phases.system_prompt:
You are a senior software engineer implementing a feature.

Context:
- Specification: {{state_dir}}/spec.md
- Epic: {{epic.id}} - {{epic.name}}
- All tickets for this epic: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets.json
- Previous implementations: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/

Document your implementation in: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/implementation.md

phases.name: Review Implementation
phases.system_prompt:
You are a code reviewer examining an implementation.

Context:
- Ticket: {{ticket.id}} - {{ticket.title}}
- Implementation notes: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/implementation.md
- Specification: {{state_dir}}/spec.md

Write your review to: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/review.md
Write follow-up tickets to: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/followups.json
phases.prompt:
Review the implementation for ticket {{ticket.id}}: {{ticket.title}}

Read the implementation notes at {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/implementation.md

Review the actual code changes and verify:
1. Acceptance criteria are met
2. Code quality is acceptable
3. No obvious bugs or issues
4. Tests are adequate

Write your review to {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/review.md

If there are issues that need fixing, create follow-up tickets in {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/followups.json with structure:
[
  {
    "id": "T001-F1",
    "title": "Fix: Issue description",
    "description": "What needs to be fixed",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "pending"
  }
]

If no issues, create an empty array: []

phases.name: Process Follow-ups
Implement Follow-up
phases.system_prompt:
You are fixing an issue identified during code review.

Original ticket: {{ticket.id}} - {{ticket.title}}
Review notes: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/review.md

Document your fix in: {{state_dir}}/epics/{{epic.id}}/tickets/{{ticket.id}}/followup-{{followup.id}}.md
